headline: MAHB Airport Privatisation Debate Returns After GMR Offer
context: India's GMR Group submitted a RM18 billion offer to acquire a 30% stake in Malaysia Airports Holdings Berhad in February 2026, reigniting debate about whether strategic national infrastructure should be privatised to foreign operators.

cards.big: RM18 billion for 30% of MAHB — foreign ownership of KL International Airport raises sovereignty alarm among 64% of Malaysians polled
cards.sub: GMR operates Delhi and Hyderabad airports — but KLIA handles 62 million passengers with national security implications

cards.big: MAHB's return on equity at 4.8% significantly underperforms Changi Airport's 12.3% and Bangkok's AOT at 9.1%
cards.sub: Foreign management expertise could unlock RM4-6 billion in annual revenue from currently undermonetised KLIA retail and cargo

cards.big: MAHB employs 8,400 Malaysians directly — privatisation template in comparable cases led to 15-25% workforce restructuring
cards.sub: Airport worker unions representing 6,200 employees formally opposed the acquisition citing job security fears

cards.big: Khazanah holds 33% of MAHB and EPF holds 12% — sale could reduce national pension fund's infrastructure portfolio
cards.sub: Cabinet approval process for strategic asset sales has no mandatory parliamentary debate requirement

cards.big: MAHB underperforms not because of ownership structure but because political appointees run a commercial operation like a government department
Should MAHB reform its board to majority-independent directors before any privatisation decision is considered?
